name: Sesame Anise Melts
recipeInstructions:
DESCRIPTION: Melt-in-your-mouth balls topped with crunchy sesame seeds are a delicious, not too sweet accompaniment to coffee or tea.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Combine flour and baking soda, set aside. Beat together butter, sugar, anise seed, and salt until light and fluffy. Beat in egg. Stir in flour gradually, blending after each addition. Divide dough into quarters and place all but one in the refrigerator. With floured hands, roll rounded teaspoons of dough into 1-inch balls. Roll each ball in sesame seeds, pressing seeds in slightly to coat all sides. Arrange balls about 1½ inches apart on greased baking sheets. Using the bottom of a glass, flatten balls to about 2/3 inch thickness. Repeat with remaining dough. Bake for 6-9 minutes or until browned around edges. Cool on racks. These cookies are fragile, so store carefully in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 10 days. Freeze for three months.
